20 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • CENTCOM Says US Marines Board Iranian Tanker RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- The US Central Command (CENTCOM) announced that US military forces boarded the Iranian-flagged commercial tanker M/T Celestial Sea in the Sea of Oman on May 20.
  • 42 US Aircraft Lost Or Damaged In Iran War, Congressional Report Reveals RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- A Congressional Research Service report released on May 13 provides the first official US government accounting of aircraft losses during Operation Epic Fury, documenting 42 airframes lost or damaged since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on February 28.
  • Nearly 90 Ships Forced To Change Course So Far, Says US Central Command RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- The United States Central Command (CENTCOM), which is responsible for American forces in the Middle East, says that US forces have forced 89 ships to change course since the implementation of the maritime blockade against Iran.

  • May 20: Iran warns against any new aggression as Pakistan ramps up diplomacy to end the war Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) on Wednesday warned that any renewed US-Israeli aggression would trigger wider-than-ever retaliation, while President Masoud Pezeshkian reiterated that the Islamic Republic will never bow to coercion.
  • Iran's Strait of Hormuz management authority defines supervisory zone Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The Iranian authority controlling the Strait of Hormuz in the Persian Gulf has defined the supervisory management zone of the waterway.
  • IRGC Navy says more ships transiting Strait of Hormuz under its coordination Press TV 20 May 2026 -- Iran reports increased ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz under new arrangements to facilitate transit via the key Persian Gulf waterway.
  • IRGC: 26 Ships Transited Strait Of Hormuz In Past 24 Hours RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy announced on May 20 that 26 commercial ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours with "coordination and security provided" by the force.
  • IRGC Says 26 Vessels Passed Through Strait of Hormuz Over Past Day Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- Twenty-six vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours, the naval forces of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said on Wednesday.

  • As Trump Speaks Of Renewed Iran Strikes, Strategic Dilemma Remains RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Over two days, US President Donald Trump has repeatedly raised the prospect that the cease-fire with Iran will be broken by new US military strikes -- in comments that have drawn close scrutiny from analysts and policymakers.
  • Trump Says Negotiations In 'Final Stages,' Threatens Renewed Attacks If Iran Talks Collapse RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says negotiations with Iran are approaching a conclusion, while warning of military action if Tehran fails to reach an agreement.

  • Qalibaf Warns US Seeking A 'New Round Of War' RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, the speaker of Iran's parliament and the top negotiator in its previous talks with Washington, claimed the United States is seeking to reignite the war against Iran.
  • Iran's Revolutionary Guards Warn Of Expanded War If Attacks Resume RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a statement saying that any resumption of US attacks on Iran will lead it to extend the war "beyond the region."
  • Iran warns war to extend beyond region if U.S., Israel repeat "aggression" People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) on Wednesday warned that if the U.S. and Israeli "aggression" against the country is repeated, the war will extend beyond the West Asia region.
  • Forcing Iran to surrender 'an illusion': President Pezeshkian Press TV 20 May 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian reiterates Iran's position that it will resist coercion aimed at forcing surrender, stressing that engagement with the Islamic Republic should instead be based on mutual respect.
  • US, Israel must find way out of 'quagmire' sooner for their own sake: Iran FM spokesman Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The United States and Israel must take responsibility for the war they started and find their own way out of the quagmire, Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman says.
  • 'War beyond the region': IRGC vows to 'bring enemies to absolute ruin' if Iran faces new aggression Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned that if aggression against Iran is repeated, the regional war that had previously been promised will this time be extended beyond the region, and that devastating blows would be delivered from places the enemy cannot imagine.

  • Strait of Hormuz conflict threatens global food prices as FAO warns time is running out FAO 20 May 2026 -- The closure of the Strait of Hormuz is not a temporary shipping disruption but the beginning of a systemic agrifood shock that could trigger a severe global food price crisis within six to 12 months. Avoiding such an outcome will require alternative trade routes, restraint on export restrictions, protection of humanitarian flows, and buffers to absorb higher transport costs, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has warned.

  • Russia's Putin, China's Xi jointly denounce recent US-Israeli war of aggression against Iran Press TV 20 May 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping have jointly condemned the recent US-Israeli war of aggression against Iran, calling for dialogue as the only way to end the destabilizing conflict.

  • Pakistan's interior minister leaves for Tehran for second time this week Press TV 20 May 2026 -- Pakistan's Interior Minister Syed Mohsin Raza Naqvi has headed to Tehran for the second time this week amid efforts to end the war between Iran and the US, Iranian news agencies report.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Senior DOW Tech Official Says Department AI Use Up 1,775% in Past Year DoD News 20 May 2026 -- During a panel today on the lethality of artificial intelligence as it relates to special operations forces, Emil Michael, undersecretary of war for research and engineering and War Department chief technology officer, said that AI use across the department is up by roughly 1,420,000 users, a 1,775% increase over the past calendar year.
  • War Department's Science Enterprise Maintains Tight Focus on Half-Dozen Key Areas DoD News 20 May 2026 -- Last year at this time, the War Department identified 14 technology areas as priorities. Since then, that list has been pared down to a more realistic, warfighter-focused set of goals, the department's chief technology officer said.
  • Senior Special Ops Leaders Discuss Value of Partnerships, Alliances DoD News 20 May 2026 -- Senior military special operations forces leaders from the U.S., Sweden and the Philippines participated in a morning panel today about the importance of SOF partnerships and alliances, as part of Special Operations Forces Week 2026 in Tampa, Florida.
  • CTF-73 Participates in SALVEX Korea 2026 US Navy 20 May 2026 -- U.S. Navy divers assigned to Mobile Diving and Salvage Unit 1 (MDSU-1) concluded Salvage Exercise (SALVEX) Korea 2026 alongside Australian maritime forces and Republic of Korea (ROK) forces at Jinhae Naval Base, April 6-10, 2026.
  • ICBM test launch verifies system reliability United States Space Force 20 May 2026 -- Air Force Global Strike Command conducted a test launch of an unarmed Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missile May 20 at 12:01 a.m. Pacific Time from Vandenberg Space Force Base, California.
  • AFRICOM commander Gen. Anderson testifies before Congress AFRICOM 20 May 2026 -- U.S. Air Force Gen. Dagvin Anderson, commander of U.S. Africa Command, testified before the U.S. House and Senate Armed Services committees May 14 and 19, 2026, about the strategic importance of Africa and evolving security threats.

  • "Semper Una": Romanian Army Participates in NATO-Serbia Exercise 2026 NATO Allied Joint Force Command (JFC) Naples 20 May 2026 -- The Romanian contingent demonstrated a firm commitment to the Alliance's values and objectives through active participation in the NATO-Serbia Exercise 2026, contributing to increased interoperability and strengthening cooperation with allied forces.
  • US Plans to Limit Military Aid Options for NATO Allies in Crises - Reports Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- The US administration plans to inform later this week its European allies in NATO of reduced military support it is ready to provide if a conflict or another major crisis occurs, Reuters reported, citing sources.
  • Secretary General highlights how Allies are stepping up ahead of Foreign Ministers meeting in Sweden NATO 20 May 2026 -- NATO Foreign Ministers will meet in Helsingborg on Thursday and Friday (21-22 May) to finalise preparations for the Ankara Summit. Speaking ahead of the meeting in Brussels on Wednesday (20 May), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte highlighted the need to deliver on the commitments made at the Summit in The Hague last year, where Allies committed to increase defence spending to 5% of GDP by 2035.
  • U.S. troop drawdown will not affect NATO defense plans, commander says People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- NATO's Supreme Allied Commander Europe Alexus Grynkewich said Tuesday that a U.S. decision to reduce its military presence in Europe by about 5,000 troops would not weaken the alliance's defense plans.

  • Russian Soldiers Place Nuclear Arms on High Alert During Drills - Belarus' Defense Ministry Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- Russian military personnel practiced bringing nuclear weapons units to the highest levels of combat readiness as part of nuclear force exercises in Belarus, the Belarusian Defense Ministry said on Wednesday.

  • Canadian armored vehicles defending Ukraine: key facts about the Roshel Senator MRAP Ukraine MOD 20 May 2026 -- Humanitarian mine clearance units have received a new batch of twenty Canadian Roshel Senator MRAP armored vehicles. The vehicles were delivered as part of a cooperation with the Demining Capability Coalition.

  • Cabinet approves NT$295 billion special budget for U.S. arms purchases CNA 20 May 2026 -- Taiwan's Cabinet on Wednesday approved a special budget proposal that allocates nearly NT$295 billion (US$9.3 billion) for a package of weapons already approved for sale to Taiwan by the United States.
  • M1A2T tanks conduct off-base exercise simulating airport defense CNA 20 May 2026 -- Six U.S.-made M1A2T Abrams tanks conducted a combat readiness exercise outside their base in northern Hsinchu County early Wednesday, simulating the defense of a nearby military airport in the event of a Chinese invasion.
  • Taiwan hopes U.S. arms sales to continue amid uncertainty: Lai CNA 20 May 2026 -- Taiwan is hoping sales of weapons by the United States will continue to support the defense capabilities it needs to maintain peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait, President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Wednesday on the second anniversary of his inauguration.

  • Middle powers unite at sea Australia DOD 20 May 2026 -- With more than a century of cooperation, Australia and Canada are continuing their strong partnership in the Indo-Pacific.
  • Apache fleet reaches six Australia DOD 20 May 2026 -- Two additional AH-64E Apache attack helicopters have been delivered to the Australian Army, marking another step in the transformation of Australia's battlefield aviation capability.

  • LAUNCH OF SANGHMITRA, YARD 3039 (NEXT GENERATION OFFSHORE PATROL VESSEL) AT M/s GRSE, KOLKATA India PIB 20 May 2026 -- Yard 3039 (Sanghmitra), Next Generation Offshore Patrol Vessel (NGOPV), was launched at M/s GRSE, Kolkata on 20 May 2026 by Mrs Sarita Vatsayan, in the presence of VAdm Sanjay Vatsayan, Vice Chief of the Naval Staff. The event was conducted with full naval honours and ceremonial splendour, reflecting the rich traditions and ethos of the Indian Navy. Senior dignitaries from the Indian Navy, Ministry of Defence, and GRSE attended the occasion.
  • IOS SAGAR (INS SUNAYNA) RETURNS TO KOCHI AFTER A LANDMARK DEPLOYMENT India PIB 20 May 2026 -- Indian Navy marked the successful culmination of the landmark IOS SAGAR deployment with the flag-in of INS Sunayna at Kochi on 20 May 2026, following an extensive multinational deployment across the Indian Ocean Region (IOR). The joint mission with 38 personnel from 16 partner nations stands as a significant demonstration of India's emergence as the Preferred Security Partner in the region and its enduring commitment towards strengthening maritime collaboration, interoperability, and capacity building amongst IOR navies.
  • Raksha Mantri holds bilateral talks with Minister of National Defence, Republic of Korea in Seoul India PIB 20 May 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh and Minister of National Defence, Republic of Korea (RoK) Mr Ahn Gyu-back held comprehensive bilateral discussions in Seoul on May 20, 2026. The two Ministers reviewed the entire spectrum of defence cooperation and discussed ways to further expand collaboration in areas such as industry, production, maritime security, emerging technologies, military exchanges, logistics & regional security.
  • MULTILATERAL EXERCISE PRAGATI 2026 COMMENCES AT UMROI, MEGHALAYA India PIB 20 May 2026 -- Multilateral military exercise PRAGATI 2026 commenced today at Umroi Military Station, Meghalaya, with the participation from 12 friendly nations, namely Bhutan, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Seychelles, Sri Lanka and Vietnam.

  • ROK military reportedly starts nuclear-powered submarine acquisition process; move risks proliferation and triggering regional arms race: Chinese experts Global Times 20 May 2026 -- According to the Yonhap News, the South Korean military has initiated the formal process to acquire nuclear-powered submarines, a Navy document showed Wednesday, amid little progress in security talks with the US over Seoul's push for the project. Chinese experts warned that the move could intensify nuclear proliferation risks, undermine the global non-proliferation regime, and heighten the possibility of a regional arms race.

  • RAF aircraft dangerously intercepted by Russian jets over Black Sea UK MOD 20 May 2026 -- UK Rivet Joint aircraft repeatedly and dangerously intercepted by two Russian jets over the Black Sea.

  • Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen: Finland will exceed NATO's 1.5% target for defence- and security-related spending in 2026 Finnish MoD 20 May 2026 -- The Ministerial Committee on Economic Policy of the Finnish Government has outlined that the expenditure Finland will be reporting under NATO's 1.5 per cent target for defence- and security-related spending will include statutory readiness and preparedness duties or capabilities critical to homeland defence that are sustained and developed by other branches of government than the Ministry of Defence.

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: Canada strengthens Estonia's security and the transatlantic bond Estonia MFA 20 May 2026 -- Today, 20 May, Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand visited Tallinn, where she met Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna, and together they officially opened the Canadian embassy in Estonia. Later in the day, a four-party meeting took place with the participation of Lithuanian Foreign Minister Kęstutis Budrys and Latvian Deputy Foreign Minister Artjoms Urulskis, who had also travelled to Estonia. The four-party meeting focused on strengthening cooperation between the Baltic States and Canada, regional security and transatlantic relations.

  • Canada invests €64 million in military infrastructure development in Latvia Latvia MOD 20 May 2026 -- On Wednesday, May 19th, foundation stones were ceremonially laid for new military infrastructure facilities in Lielvārde and Riga, officially marking the start of construction on two major projects worth €64 million.

  • Leadership of Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense visits Türkiye Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 20 May 2026 -- Minister of Defense of the Republic of Azerbaijan, Colonel General Zakir Hasanov; First Deputy Minister of Defense - Chief of the General Staff of the Azerbaijan Army, Colonel General Karim Valiyev; Deputy Minister of Defense - Commander of the Air Force, Lieutenant General Namig Islamzade; along with other senior officials of the Ministry, are on a working visit to Türkiye to participate in the Distinguished Visitors Day of the "EFES-2026" multinational joint exercise, Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • A look at the Defence news 11 - 17 May Netherlands Ministry of Defence 20 May 2026

  • The Chief of Defence Staff, at the NATO Military Committee and EU Military Committee Meetings Romania - Ministry of National Defence 20 May 2026 -- The Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, participated, on May 19 and 20, in Brussels, in the NATO Military Committee and the European Union Military Committee Meetings, two of the most important consultation and strategic coordination formats at the level of the chiefs of defence of Allied and European Union member states.

  • PAF probes crash of trainer aircraft in Benguet Philippine News Agency 20 May 2026 -- A trainer aircraft of the Philippine Air Force (PAF) crashed in Benguet province on Wednesday morning, killing its two pilots.
  • PAF boosts rapid deployment capability with 2 new choppers Philippine News Agency 20 May 2026 -- The Philippine Air Force (PAF) on Wednesday said its tactical airlift and disaster response capabilities received a major boost following the formal commissioning of two brand new Bell 412EPX utility helicopters.

  • Shaheen, Warren Ask Rubio and Bessent to Explain Sanctions Relief for Venezuela Regime Head Delcy Rodríguez US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 20 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), Ranking Member of the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee, sent a letter to Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Secretary of Treasury Scott Bessent demanding an explanation for the Trump Administration's decision to remove sanctions on Delcy Rodríguez, acting President of Venezuela and a key actor in Nicolas Maduro's repressive regime.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senator Cruz Introduce Bipartisan Bill Responding to Democratic Backsliding and Political Repression in Tanzania US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 20 May 2026 -- Yesterday, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Ted Cruz (R-TX) introduced the bipartisan Reassessing the United States-Tanzania Bilateral Relationship Act.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for May 20, 2026 DoD
  • Two Defense Contractors Arrested for Bribery and Major Fraud Conspiracy Scheme Affecting Department of War Technology Innovation Contracts US Dept. of Justice 20 May 2026 -- Today, the Justice Department announced criminal charges against Leonard Pick, 62, of Palm Beach Shores, Florida, and Brian Kent, 59, of Tampa, Florida, for orchestrating a bribery and major fraud conspiracy that corrupted the competitive procurement process for a Department of War technology innovation lab in the Pacific.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 20 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 20 May 2026
  • Security Council reform 'is absolutely essential': Guterres UN News 20 May 2026 -- The UN Secretary-General on Wednesday underscored the need to effectively re-open the Strait of Hormuz and expand UN Security Council membership to curb the impunity of veto-wielding "superpowers".

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 20 May 2026 -- "Israel has every right to prevent provocative flotillas of Hamas terrorist supporters from entering our territorial waters and reaching Gaza. However, the way that Minister Ben Gvir dealt with the flotilla activists is not in line with Israel's values and norms."
  • PM Netanyahu in a meeting with fighters on the Eastern border Israel - Prime Minister 20 May 2026

  • Commander of Israel's 401st Armoured Brigade seriously injured in Hezbollah drone operation Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The commander of the 401st Armoured Brigade of the Israeli military has been seriously injured in a drone operation conducted by Hezbollah resistance fighters against the occupation forces' positions in southern Lebanon.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Israel's decision to establish military facilities at the UNRWA Sheikh Jarrah compound UN Secretary-General 20 May 2026
  • Guterres condemns Israeli move to militarise seized compound in occupied East Jerusalem UN News 20 May 2026 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Wednesday strongly condemned Israel's decision to establish military facilities at a seized UNRWA compound in occupied East Jerusalem, calling the move "wholly unacceptable".
  • Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon's first responders under fire UN News 20 May 2026 -- Before heading to strike sites in war-torn Lebanon, rescue workers and paramedics often say goodbye to one another - a ritual captured in widely shared videos reflecting the growing dangers faced by aid workers since hostilities between Hezbollah and Israel erupted on 2 March.
  • UNRWA Situation Report #10 on the Lebanon Emergency Response 2026 (7 May 2026) UNRWA 20 May 2026
  • UNRWA Situation Report #221 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Gaza Strip and the Occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem UNRWA 20 May 2026

  • OIC Condemns Media Reports Regarding the Opening of an Embassy by the so-called "Somaliland" in Occupied Al-Quds Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 20 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed its grave concern and strong condemnation of media reports regarding the intention of the so-called "Somaliland" to open a so-called "embassy" in occupied Al-Quds (Jerusalem), in flagrant defiance of the will of the entire international community and in grave violation of the principles of the United Nations Charter and relevant UN resolutions.
  • Arab League condemns Somaliland's plan to open embassy in occupied al-Quds Press TV 20 May 2026 -- The Arab League has denounced the decision by the breakaway region of Somaliland to open an embassy in the Israeli-occupied al-Quds, describing the move as "absolutely unacceptable."
  • The OIC Condemns the Israeli Occupation's Attack on the Al-Sumoud Flotilla Heading to Gaza Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 20 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) expressed its strong condemnation of the crime of aggression, violence, and humiliation committed by the Israeli occupation forces, under the supervision of the extremist "National Security Minister" Itamar Ben-Gvir, against the Al-Sumoud Flotilla ("Flotilla of Resilience"), which was carrying international activists and urgent humanitarian and relief aid to the Gaza Strip.
  • MFA Statement Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026 -- The Polish Ministry of Foreign Affairs is following with great concern the actions taken by the Israeli armed forces against activists participating in the Global Sumud Flotilla, which declares to be delivering humanitarian aid to the Gaza Strip.
  • South Korea's president condemns Israel's kidnapping of nationals on Gaza aid flotilla Press TV 20 May 2026 -- South Korean President Lee Jae-Myung has sharply condemned Israel's abduction of South Korean nationals aboard a Gaza-bound aid ship, calling the act "inhumane" and suggesting that Seoul should arrest Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he enters South Korean territory.
  • No: 97, 20 May 2026, Regarding the Use of Violence by an Israeli Minister Against Detained Participants of the Global Sumud Flotilla Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Protection of Civilians, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine and Sudan UNOCHA 20 May 2026 -- UN, NGO leaders call for on Member States to uphold international law

  • Calls to Uphold International Law Dominate Security Council Debate on Civilian Protection, as Delegates Demand Safety for Journalists, Aid Workers UN Security Council 20 May 2026 -- While civilians continue to suffer because parties to conflict choose to ignore their legal obligation to protect them, the Security Council heard today that adhering to the rules of war — and enforcing accountability when they are broken — can produce a different result.
  • UN heads condemn failure to protect civilians from growing threats to their security UNOCHA 20 May 2026 -- Statement by Principals of the Inter-Agency Standing Committee
  • OCHA tells Security Council protecting civilians "cannot be outsourced, postponed, or diluted" UNOCHA 20 May 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the protection of civilians in armed conflict by Edem Wosornu, Director, Crisis Response Division for OCHA, on behalf of Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • We call on all parties to conflict to protect civilians: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 20 May 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Protection of civilians in armed conflict.
  • Note to Correspondents: Declaration reaffirming the principles of the Joint UN-AU Framework for Enhanced Partnership in Peace and Security UN Secretary-General 20 May 2026
  • Secretary-General's video message to the second Ministerial Conference on Peacekeeping in the Francophone Area UN Secretary-General 20 May 2026

  • DND rejects China's opposition to PH infra works in WPS Philippine News Agency 20 May 2026 -- The Department of National Defense (DND) on Wednesday rejected China's latest statement opposing Philippine infrastructure and development activities on Pag-asa and Lawak Islands in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).

  • 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens UN News 20 May 2026 -- Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

News Reports

  • Statement by the Revolutionary Government Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026 -- "The Revolutionary Government condemns in the strongest terms the despicable accusation by the United States Department of Justice announced on May 20 and proclaimed for several weeks against Army General Raúl Castro Ruz, leader of the Cuban Revolution."
  • United States Unseals Superseding Indictment Charging Raul Castro and Five Castro Regime Co-Defendants for 1996 Shoot-Down of Brothers to the Rescue Aircraft US Dept. of Justice 20 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of Justice today announced the unsealing of a superseding indictment charging Raul Modesto Castro Ruz, 94, of Holguin, Cuba; along with Lorenzo Alberto Perez-Perez of Las Tunas, Cuba; Emilio José Palacio Blanco; José Fidel Gual Barzaga; Raul Simanca Cardenas; and Luis Raul Gonzalez-Pardo Rodriguez, for their alleged roles in the Feb. 24, 1996 shoot-down of two unarmed U.S. civilian aircraft operated by Brothers to the Rescue (BTTR), also known as Hermanos al Rescate, over international waters.
  • Cuban Foreign Minister Accuses Rubio of Lying About Causes of Crisis on Island Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez Parilla on Wednesday accused US Secretary of State Marco Rubio of lying about the true causes behind the longstanding hardships affecting the island.
  • Secretary Rubio's Message to the Cuban People on Their Independence Day US Dept. of State 20 May 2026

  • WHO says Ebola outbreak not "pandemic emergency" People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- The risk of the Ebola epidemic is "high at the national and regional levels, and low at the global level," and the situation is not a "pandemic emergency," the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday.
  • Ebola risk is high inside DR Congo but it's no pandemic emergency: WHO UN News 20 May 2026 -- The deadly Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda does not represent a global pandemic emergency, although the risk is high at a regional and national level, the UN health agency chief said on Wednesday.
  • Ebola outbreak: 'Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community' UN News 20 May 2026 -- Facing a fast-moving Ebola outbreak caused by a rare strain of the virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) is relying on a rapid, community-centred response to halt transmission in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a senior official has told UN News.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Statement on Ebola Outbreak and the Cost of President Trump's Retreat on Global Health US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 20 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statement on the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. Hundreds have been infected, including an American doctor, and over 100 have died. The World Health Organization has declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern.

  • Sanctioning Two Sinaloa Cartel Networks for Trafficking Illicit Fentanyl US Dept. of State 20 May 2026 -- The United States is sanctioning two networks linked to the Sinaloa Cartel responsible for narcotics trafficking and money laundering operations.
  • Treasury Disrupts Sinaloa Cartel Narco-Terrorist Fentanyl Trafficking Operations US Treasury 20 May 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned more than a dozen individuals and entities, comprising two distinct networks, linked to the terrorist Sinaloa Cartel and its fentanyl trafficking activities.
  • Six Russians Jailed in Armenia on Flimsy Espionage Allegations - Report Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- Armenia continues to hold six Russian citizens on espionage-related charges linked to alleged filming activities for Azerbaijan, despite what their supporters describe as weak evidence and the absence of any classified material, RT reports.

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's answer to a question from the Moscow. Kremlin. Putin programme, Beijing, May 20, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026
  • Kyrgyzstan Shuts 50 Companies In New Crackdown On Russia-Related Sanctions Evasion RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Kyrgyzstan has taken sweeping action to clamp down on companies suspected of circumventing Western sanctions imposed on Russia following its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

  • Putin Gets Show Of Unity, But No New Pipeline Deal In Beijing Summit RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese leader Xi Jinping signed a series of agreements in Beijing aimed at deepening strategic and economic ties, as Moscow sought to turn the optics of the summit into tangible gains on energy cooperation amid turmoil in global oil and gas markets caused by the war in Iran.
  • Russia-China talks President of Russia 20 May 2026 -- The People's Assembly House hosted Vladimir Putin's talks with President of China Xi Jinping.
  • Press statements following Russia-China talks President of Russia 20 May 2026 -- Following the talks, Vladimir Putin and President of the People's Republic of China Xi Jinping made statements for the media.
  • Tea reception with Xi Jinping President of Russia 20 May 2026 -- President Vladimir Putin's visit to China concluded with a tea reception with President of the People's Republic of China Xi Jinping.
  • Meeting with Premier of the State Council of China Li Qiang President of Russia 20 May 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting with Premier of the State Council of the People's Republic of China Li Qiang.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with Shanghai Media Group, Moscow, May 20, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026
  • Russia-China Alliance Grows Stronger Despite Western Pressure - Lavrov Sputnik 20 May 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said Moscow and Beijing have built a strategic partnership strong enough to withstand Western pressure, pointing to expanding energy cooperation, growing technological coordination and a trade turnover exceeding $200 billion.
  • Putin, Xi Hold Beijing Summit With Energy, Iran In Focus RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin met Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 20 for talks centered on deepening energy ties and presenting a united front against what both leaders cast as a Western-dominated global order.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 20, 2026 PRC MofA 20 May 2026

  • Chinese defense ministry slams Lai Ching-te's secessionist fallacies under guise of "peace" PRC MND 20 May 2026 -- A Chinese defense spokesperson on Wednesday denounced Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te's aggressive peddling of "Taiwan independence" fallacies under the guise of so-called "peace."
  • Two years in, Lai faces new hurdles in overseas travel CNA 20 May 2026 -- Beijing is unlikely to again block an overseas trip by President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) as it did when he tried to visit Eswatini, but Lai may not secure a stopover in the contiguous United States anytime soon, analysts said at the halfway mark of Lai's first term in office.
  • Lai, KMT trade barbs over cross-strait policy differences CNA 20 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德), who also chairs the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP), and the main opposition Kuomintang (KMT) traded barbs on Wednesday over the two parties' cross-strait policies, with Lai accusing the KMT of undermining Taiwan's sovereignty and the KMT criticizing Lai for engaging in a smear campaign.
  • Placing ambitions for 'independence' above welfare of Taiwan residents entirely self-deceptive, miscalculates situation: MND on Lai's May 20 speech Global Times 20 May 2026 -- Facing the inevitable trend of national reunification and the profound shifts in the international landscape, Lai Ching-te remains obsessed with the illusion of "seeking independence by relying on external support," Jiang Bin, spokesperson for the Ministry of National Defense said in response to a media query about Lai's Wednesday speech claiming that maintaining peace and stability across the Taiwan Straits is Taiwan's "strategic goal," while calling for the strengthening of asymmetric military capabilities and enhancing "sustainable national defense capabilities."
  • Lai's May 20 speech sparks broad condemnation; claims rife with lies and deception, proving himself a saboteur of cross-Straits peace: spokespersons Global Times 20 May 2026 -- Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te's speech on Wednesday marking his second year in office drew criticism from China's Foreign Ministry, the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council and China's Ministry of National Defense on the same day, with spokespersons saying the remarks are rife with lies and deception and exposed Lai's ugly true nature as a saboteur of cross-Straits peace.
  • Chinese FM slams Lai's rhetoric related to arms purchasing, says attempts to seek independence by relying on external support are 'nothing but wishful thinking' Global Times 20 May 2026 -- In response to rhetoric by Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te expressing hope to continue U.S. arms purchases, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wednesday that Taiwan is an inalienable part of China's territory. It has never been a country and will never be a country. The Lai Ching-te authorities have developed a conditioned response of making every attempt to grab attention, which only time and again revealed their true nature as provocateurs and saboteurs of peace.
  • Lai is saboteur of cross Straits peace and a creator of crises in Taiwan Straits, Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson says on Lai's May 20 speech Global Times 20 May 2026 -- Regarding cross-Straits ties related contents in Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te's May 20 speech and media briefing, Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday that Lai's speech is rife with lies and deception, hostility and confrontation.
  • Mainland says Lai Ching-te's separatist remarks reveal "weak and deceitful" nature People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Wednesday said that Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te's recent separatist remarks on social media fully exposed his bluster and weakness, and were filled with lies and hostility.
  • Mainland refutes Lai Ching-te's latest remarks, warns against secessionist moves People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Wednesday slammed Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te over his latest remarks on cross-Strait relations, accusing him of promoting secessionism and escalating tensions across the Taiwan Strait.
  • Full text of President Lai's address on 2nd anniversary of taking office CNA 20 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) delivered an address marking the second anniversary of his presidency on Wednesday.
  • Taiwan's future not to be decided by 'external force': President Lai CNA 20 May 2026 -- The future of Taiwan must not be decided by an "external force" or hijacked by fear or division, President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Wednesday on the second anniversary of his inauguration.
  • China firmly opposes official interaction between US and Taiwan region, opposes US arms sales to Taiwan: spokesperson Global Times 20 May 2026 -- "We firmly oppose any form of official interaction between the US and Taiwan region of China, and firmly oppose US arms sales to Taiwan region of China. This position is consistent and clear," Zhu Fenglian, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said at a press briefing on Wednesday.
  • State Council Taiwan Affairs Office responds to Trump's Taiwan-related remarks of 'not looking to have somebody go independent' Global Times 20 May 2026
  • Lai's 'five-point statement' fully exposes true nature of being outwardly aggressive but inwardly weak, his cunning, despicable character: mainland spokesperson Global Times 20 May 2026
  • Lai's 'incompetence in governance, expertise in infighting' seriously deviates from mainstream public opinion on island: mainland spokesperson on impeachment motion against Lai Global Times 20 May 2026
  • DPP authorities cling to 'Taiwan independence' separatism while resorting to wordplay to deceive people on island and intl community: Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson Global Times 20 May 2026
  • Attempting to 'crash the meeting" to seek attention only brings self-inflicted humiliation: mainland spokesperson responds to DPP's hype about WHA Global Times 20 May 2026 -- The DPP authorities are hyping up issues related to the World Health Assembly (WHA), using the guise of health affairs to engage in "independence" political manipulation, and have no concern for the interests and well-being of the residents of Taiwan island, Zhu Fenglian, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said at a regular press conference on Wednesday.
  • New vision of China-US relations clarifies direction for two countries to properly manage differences on Taiwan question: spokesperson Global Times 20 May 2026
  • Mainland spokesperson responds to question regarding possibility of more cross-Straits exchanges after China-US summit Global Times 20 May 2026
  • WHA's rejection of Taiwan-related proposal fully shows unshakable support of int'l community for one-China principle: spokesperson People's Daily 20 May 2026 -- The 79th World Health Assembly (WHA) decided once again not to include in its agenda a so-called Taiwan-related proposal, which fully shows that the international community's support for the one-China principle is unshakable, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said on Tuesday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 20 May 2026

  • Satellite imagery shows new North Korea-Russia bridge nearing completion Radio Free Asia 20 May 2026 -- Satellite imagery has revealed that North Korea and Russia are scrambling to complete a new high-capacity road bridge across their Tumen River border, and experts told Radio Free Asia that the fervor for completing the project quickly is a sign of increased cooperation and warm relations between Pyongyang and Moscow.
  • First-stage Construction of Tanchon Power Station Completed in DPRK KCNA 20 May 2026 -- The first-stage construction of the Tanchon Power Station, which is of great significance in the development of the country's electric power industry, has been successfully completed.
  • Name of Pleasure Park Given by Respected Comrade General Secretary Kim Jong Un KCNA 20 May 2026 -- One day in April 2012, the respected Comrade General Secretary Kim Jong Un visited the Rungna People's Pleasure Park under construction.

  • Iran Imports Raw Materials For Petrochemical Sector Damaged By War RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Iran's Industry, Mines, and Trade Ministry has for the first time permitted the import of some raw materials related to the petrochemical and polymer sectors through sailor and courier procedures.
  • Iran's Economy Is Collapsing, Says US Ambassador To UN RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- Mike Waltz, the US ambassador to the United Nations, said the Iranian government's financial resources are "running out" and that the country's economy is "in a state of collapse."

  • FM Sa'ar addresses the Israel-Czech Business Forum in Prague along with Czech Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Petr Macinka Israel MFA 20 May 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: "The forum reflects our intent to strengthen the strategic partnership between our governments and longstanding friendship between our peoples."

  • Prime Minister meets with Prime Minister of Italy (May 20, 2026) India MEA 20 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held official talks with Prime Minister of the Italian Republic, H.E. Ms. Giorgia Meloni in Rome on May 20, 2026.
  • Prime Minister calls on the President of Italy (May 20, 2026) India MEA 20 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi called on H.E. Mr. Sergio Mattarella, the President of the Italian Republic, at Quirinale, the Presidential Palace in Rome today.
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the visit of the Prime Minister to Italy (May 20, 2026) India MEA 20 May 2026

  • Remarks by the DPM/FM before adoption of Resolution by the Senate of Pakistan Commemorating 75th Anniversary of Establishment of Diplomatic Relations between Pakistan and China , 20 May 2026 at Senate of Pakistan Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026
  • Russia-Pakistan Joint Press Release: 16th Meeting of the Russia-Pakistan Consultative Group on Strategic Stability Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 May 2026

  • Joint Statement of the Fourth Canada-Baltics "3+1" Foreign Ministers' Meeting Canada 20 May 2026

  • "We will make the great Türkiye the shining star of this century" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 20 May 2026 -- In a speech at AK Party's group meeting, President Erdoğan, addressing the youth, said: "The new Türkiye will be your creation. The great and strong Türkiye will rise on your shoulders. To this end, we will be one, united and powerful. Together, we will make the great Türkiye the shining star of this century."

  • 'Ambiguity Is Dangerous': Don Bacon Warns Strains In Western Alliance Could Benefit Russia, Iran, And China RFE/RL 20 May 2026 -- US Congressman Don Bacon, a senior Republican on the House Armed Services Committee, has warned in a wide-ranging interview that uncertainty from Washington risks emboldening adversaries, including Russia, Iran, and China, amid growing strains within the NATO alliance.

  • World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees UN News 20 May 2026 -- The Representative of the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, in Ukraine has strongly condemned the deadly and destructive Russian missile and drone strikes in Dnipro on Tuesday night.
